originalTitle: Deadly Sudan drone strike targets funeral procession
title: Reporter Rafiki Says: Let's Talk About Being Safe in Sudan
content:
Hello, My Friends! I'm Reporter Rafiki!

Hello, hello, wonderful children of Africa and beyond! It's me, Reporter Rafiki, your friend who loves to share news from our amazing continent. I wear my special safari hat and always have a big smile! Today, we're going to talk about something important. News helps us learn about our world and how we can all be kind to each other. Even when news sounds a bit sad, we can always find ways to hope for good things.

Let's Talk About Sudan

Africa is a big, beautiful continent! It has so many countries, like Ghana, Kenya, South Africa, and also Sudan! Sudan is a very large country in East Africa. It has sunny deserts and a long, important river called the Nile. Did you know? Sudan is famous for its ancient pyramids, just like Egypt! They are very old and tell stories of kings and queens from long, long ago. People in Sudan are like people everywhere. They have families, go to school, play games, and work hard. They love their country and their communities.

What Happened in El-Obeid?

Sometimes, grown-ups in some parts of the world have big disagreements. When this happens, they can start to fight. This is very, very sad, my friends. Fighting makes everyone unhappy. In a city in Sudan called El-Obeid, some sad things happened. People there were walking together. They were going to a special event to say goodbye to someone who had passed away. This is called a funeral procession. While they were walking quietly, a big flying machine, like a robot, made a loud noise. Some people got hurt. It was a very sad and scary day for the community in El-Obeid. Did you know? El-Obeid is a very important city in Sudan. It's known for its gum arabic, which comes from trees and is used in many foods and sweets!

What is a Funeral Procession?

When someone we love very much has to go to sleep forever, we feel very sad. We miss them a lot. A funeral is a time when family and friends come together to remember that person. They might tell happy stories, sing gentle songs, or just be quiet together. Walking together in a funeral procession is a way to show respect and love. It's a very peaceful and important time for a community to be together and comfort each other. It is never okay for fighting to happen during such a special and sad time. Everyone should feel safe when they are saying goodbye to someone they love.

Why is Fighting Sad?

Imagine if you and your best friend started to fight all the time. It wouldn't be fun, right? Fighting makes people unhappy. When grown-ups fight in a country, it can cause many problems. Homes can get broken, and schools might close. Children might not be able to go to school or play safely outside. People might not have enough food or water. It stops people from living happy and safe lives. Did you know? When people fight, it often makes them feel scared and worried. That's why being kind and friendly is so much better for everyone!

What is a Drone?

We heard about a big flying robot, or a drone, that was involved. What is a drone? A drone is like a small airplane or a big toy helicopter, but it doesn't have a pilot sitting inside. Some drones are used for good things! They can take amazing pictures from high up in the sky. Some drones help farmers check their fields. Other drones can even deliver important medicines to places that are far away. But sometimes, people use drones for fighting, and that makes us sad. We wish all drones could only be used for helpful and happy things, like flying a camera to see a beautiful African sunset!

Why is Peace Important?

My friends, peace means everyone is safe, happy, and can play together without worrying. It means no fighting, just smiles, hugs, and friendship. When there is peace, children can go to school every day. Families can stay together and help build their homes and villages. Peace helps everyone to learn, grow, and be the best they can be. It's like when everyone in your classroom is friends, sharing toys and helping each other. That's peace! Did you know? The word 'peace' is celebrated all over the world! People often use a white dove or an olive branch as symbols of peace, meaning no fighting.

What Can We Do?

Even though we might be far away from Sudan, we can still do something! We can send our kindest wishes and hopes for peace to all the children and families in Sudan. We can also learn more about Sudan and all the amazing countries in Africa. The more we learn, the more we understand each other. And understanding helps us to be friends! Most importantly, we can be kind to our friends and family every single day. Every kind word, every shared toy, every gentle hug helps to build peace in our own homes and communities. Being kind is a super superpower! Did you know? Even a small act of kindness, like sharing your snack or helping a friend, can make a big difference in someone's day!

Let's Hope for Sunshine!

Reporter Rafiki knows that even when sad things happen, there is always hope. We hope for peace and safety for all the children and grown-ups in Sudan. We want them to be able to play, learn, and live happily. Africa is a continent full of strong, kind, and wonderful people. Let's all hope that the sunshine of peace will soon shine brightly over every part of Sudan. Keep smiling and keep being kind, my friends! Reporter Rafiki sends you big, warm hugs! Bye for now!
summary: Reporter Rafiki shares news about a sad event in El-Obeid, Sudan, where people got hurt during a funeral procession, reminding us that fighting is sad and peace is important for everyone to be safe and happy.
whyMatters: This news is important because it reminds us that fighting causes sadness and hurts people, especially in places like Sudan, and it teaches us why it's so important for everyone to live in peace and safety.

originalTitle: Nigeria evacuates citizens from South Africa as anti-migrant sentiment rises
title: Friends Coming Home: Nigeria Helps Its People
content:
Hello, super kids! Your friendly news reporter is here with an important story from our big, beautiful continent, Africa! Imagine if your friend went to another town, and then they needed help to come back home. That's a bit like what's happening with Nigeria and South Africa.

Nigeria Helps Its Friends Come Home

Nigeria is a very big country in Africa. South Africa is another big country, also in Africa. Some people from Nigeria live and work or study in South Africa. They are like guests or friends visiting another place. But sometimes, things can get a little tricky. Our friends from Nigeria who were in South Africa needed some help to come back home. So, Nigeria sent big airplanes to bring them safely back!

Why Are People Coming Home?

You might wonder, 'Why did they need to come home?' Well, sadly, some people in South Africa were not very kind to visitors from other countries, like Nigeria. This is a big word, but it means people were being unkind just because someone was from a different country. It's like not wanting to play with someone just because they have a different favorite color, but much, much sadder and more serious. When people are unkind to those from other countries, it makes everyone sad. Did you know? Africa has 54 countries! That's a lot of different people, languages, and cultures, all living together on one amazing continent.

Being Kind to Everyone

It's really important to be kind to everyone, isn't it? No matter if they come from the next village, the next town, or a country far away. This unkindness is sometimes called 'xenophobia.' It's a hard word, but it just means being mean to strangers or people from other lands. Our families teach us to share, to play nicely, and to treat everyone with love. This is true for big countries too. Countries should be friendly neighbours. They should welcome people from other places and treat them with respect and kindness.

Why Do People Travel to Other Countries?

People often travel from one country to another for many reasons. Maybe they want to find a new job to help their families. Maybe they want to go to a special school or university to learn new things. Or maybe they have family living there and want to visit them. Imagine you want to learn how to fix cars, and there's a really good car school in another town. You might travel there, right? It's the same for adults moving between countries. They hope to find new opportunities and a good life. Did you know? Nigeria is the country with the most people in all of Africa! It's like a really, really big family!

Nigeria's Special Airplanes

To bring their citizens home, Nigeria used special airplanes. These airplanes flew all the way to South Africa. They picked up the Nigerians who wanted to come home and brought them back safely to their families. It was like a big, helpful rescue mission! It shows that countries care about their people, no matter where they are in the world. When you're in trouble or need help, your home country wants to help you. It's a way for a country to show love and care for its citizens.

Working Together in Africa

Africa is a continent of many beautiful countries. We all share this amazing land. It's like a big neighborhood. In a neighborhood, we want everyone to be friends and help each other. We want to live in peace. When people are unkind to visitors, it hurts our whole African family. We should all try to understand each other and be good friends. Sharing smiles and friendly greetings makes everyone feel happy and safe. Did you know? The longest river in the world, the Nile River, flows through many countries in Africa!

What Can We Learn?

So, what can we learn from this story, super kids? We learn that kindness is very important. We should always be friendly to new people, even if they look different, talk differently, or come from another place. Imagine if you moved to a new school. You would want new friends to be kind to you, wouldn't you? It's the same for people from one country visiting another. Let's all promise to be kind and welcoming to everyone! Our big African family should always be a place where everyone feels safe and loved. Remember, a kind heart makes the world a better place for all of us!
summary: Nigeria helped its citizens come home from South Africa because some people there were unkind to visitors from other countries.
whyMatters: This matters because everyone should be kind to each other, no matter where they come from, and countries should help their people when they need it, so everyone can feel safe and loved.

originalTitle: Artan to referee Uefa Super Cup after losing World Cup spot
title: Artan to referee Uefa Super Cup after losing World Cup spot
content: Somali referee Omar Artan is appointed to take charge of the Uefa Super Cup match between Paris St-Germain and Aston Villa later this summer after being denied a spot at the World Cup.

title: Super Teams Make Our African Homes Sparkle!
content:
Hello, Future Superstars!

Hello, hello, my wonderful friends across Africa! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, ready to share some exciting news! Today, we're going to talk about something super important: how wonderful things happen in our towns and villages because people work together. Imagine a big, beautiful puzzle. Each person is a piece, and when we all fit together, we make something amazing! Sometimes, grown-ups talk about important people who help make big decisions for our country. These are like the super helpers behind the scenes. They work with our leaders to make sure everything runs smoothly and that everyone, especially kids like you, has what they need to grow up happy and strong. It's all about teamwork!

What is a Leader?

First, let's talk about leaders! What is a leader? A leader is a person who helps guide a group of people. Think of your teacher in school. They are a leader because they guide you in learning. Or maybe your older brother or sister, who helps lead the games you play. In our towns and countries, we have special leaders too! These could be a village chief, a mayor of a city, or even the President of a whole country. Our leaders have very important jobs. They think about what everyone needs and how to make things better for all of us. They listen to people and try to solve problems. It's a big job, like being the captain of a football team or the conductor of a big orchestra. They need many people to help them achieve great things. Did you know? Many African countries have traditional leaders, like chiefs or elders, who have guided their communities for hundreds of years! They often work with modern government leaders to make sure everyone is heard.

Who Helps Our Leaders?

Now, here's the secret! Even the strongest leaders need help. They have teams of people who work with them every single day. These helpers are super important! They might not be chosen by everyone in an election, but they are chosen by the leader because they are clever, hardworking, and want to help our communities grow. Think about building a new school. The leader might say, "We need a new school here!" But it's the helpers who draw the plans, find the builders, and make sure there are enough bricks and wood. They are like the busy bees in a hive, making sure all the honey is made! These helpers are like the unseen superheroes who make sure decisions turn into real things. They are very powerful because they help to make sure that the good plans of our leaders actually happen. They work quietly, but their work makes a huge difference in our lives. Did you know? In ancient Egypt, pharaohs (who were like kings) had many advisors and scribes who helped them manage their vast kingdom! This idea of helpers is very old!

Making Our Homes and Towns Great!

So, what kinds of amazing things do our leaders and their helpers do? Lots and lots! They help build new roads so that it's easier to travel between towns. They make sure there is clean water for everyone to drink, which is super important for staying healthy. They also help build hospitals so that when we feel sick, we can get better quickly. Imagine a town where all the children have a safe place to play, with swings and slides. Our leaders and their teams work to create these fun parks! They also help make sure there are enough teachers and books in our schools, so you can learn all about the world and become even smarter. Every day, decisions are made that affect your life. From the food you eat to the clothes you wear, and even the air you breathe, someone, somewhere, is working hard to make sure our communities are safe, healthy, and happy. It takes a big team to make a big difference!

From Clean Water to Happy Schools

Let's think about clean water. Water is life, isn't it? Our leaders and their expert helpers work tirelessly to find sources of clean water, build pipes to bring it to our homes, and make sure it stays safe to drink. They also teach communities about why clean water is so important. And schools! Learning is a superpower, and our leaders know it. They work with their teams to build new schools, hire wonderful teachers, and make sure there are school supplies for everyone. They want every child in Africa to have the chance to learn and shine brightly. These teams also help with other important things, like growing food for everyone. They work with farmers to make sure crops grow well and are shared fairly. They also help protect our beautiful environment, like the forests and rivers, so that future generations can enjoy them too. It’s all about building a good life for everyone! Did you know? Some countries in Africa have special programs to make sure every child gets at least one nutritious meal at school! This helps them learn better.

Protecting Our Wild Animals!

Africa is famous for its incredible wild animals, like elephants, lions, and giraffes! Our leaders and their helpers also work very hard to protect these magnificent creatures and their homes. They create special places called national parks and reserves, where animals can live safely without being disturbed. They also work with park rangers, who are like superheroes for animals! These rangers protect animals from harm and make sure their habitats are safe. This is so important because these animals are a special part of Africa's beauty and wonder. It's a huge team effort to keep our wildlife safe and thriving for you to enjoy. Imagine if these teams didn't work together! Our animals might not be safe, and our beautiful natural places might disappear. It's a big job, but with everyone helping, we can keep Africa's natural treasures safe. Did you know? The Serengeti National Park in Tanzania and Kenya is home to one of the greatest wildlife shows on Earth, the Great Migration, where millions of animals travel across the land!

How You Can Be a Helper Too!

So, my young friends, you might be thinking, "How can *I* be a helper?" Even though you're not a grown-up leader or a special advisor, you can still be a super helper in your own way! You can help at home by cleaning your room or helping with chores. You can help at school by listening to your teacher, being kind to your friends, and keeping your classroom tidy. Even small acts of kindness and responsibility make a big difference. When you throw your litter in the bin, you're helping to keep your community clean. When you share your toys, you're helping to make friends happy. Every little bit helps to make our communities better places for everyone. Remember, just like the grown-up teams help our leaders, you can be a helper for your family, your school, and your community. Your positive actions create ripples of goodness!

Working Together Makes Us Strong!

So, there you have it, amazing kids! From the big leaders in our countries to the important helpers who work with them, and even to you, we all play a part in making Africa a wonderful place to live. It's all about teamwork, kindness, and making good choices for everyone. These 'behind-the-scenes' helpers might not always be famous, but their hard work is what turns good ideas into real improvements for all of us. They are a big reason why our schools are built, our water is clean, and our wild animals are safe. When we all work together, we can achieve anything! Keep shining brightly, Africa's future is in your hands!
summary: This news report for African kids explains how leaders and their unseen teams work together to create good things like schools, clean water, and safe environments for people and animals across Africa, showing how teamwork makes communities sparkle.
whyMatters: This matters because it helps us understand that many people work together to make our lives better, even if we don't always see them. It shows that teamwork is very important for our communities to grow strong and happy, and that everyone, even kids, can be a helper.

originalTitle: Bahrain releases footage of damage caused by intercepted Iranian drones
title: Bahrain releases footage of damage caused by intercepted Iranian drones
content: Bahrain released video of damage in Manama after debris from intercepted Iranian drones fell in populated areas

originalTitle: Lebanon Latest: Ceasefire talks continue under fire
title: Let's Talk About Peace in Lebanon!
content:
Hello, amazing friends all across Africa! It's your friendly news reporter, Kiki the Kind, here to share some important news from far away. Today, we're talking about a place called Lebanon. It's a country, just like your country, but it's on a different big piece of land called a continent, far away from Africa. Think of it like a neighbor, but a very, very distant one!

What's Happening in Lebanon?

Sometimes, grown-ups have big disagreements. It's a bit like when you and your friend might argue over a toy, but much, much bigger. In Lebanon, some groups of people are having a very big disagreement, and sometimes it makes things difficult and not peaceful. There are two groups of people who are not agreeing, and they are Israel and some people in southern Lebanon. When grown-ups don't agree, sometimes they move around in the area where the problem is. This is what we call 'ground operations' – it just means people are moving on the land.

What is a 'Ceasefire'?

Imagine you and your friend are having a little argument. What's the best thing to do? You might say, "Let's have a time-out! Let's stop shouting and take a break." A ceasefire is like a giant time-out for grown-ups who are disagreeing. It means that everyone agrees to stop fighting for a while. It's a promise to be quiet and peaceful. This gives everyone a chance to calm down and talk things over. It's the first step to making things better and bringing peace. Did you know? The word 'cease' means to stop, and 'fire' here means fighting. So 'ceasefire' literally means 'stop fighting'!

Why Are People Talking?

While this big disagreement is happening, many kind people and other countries are trying to help. They want everyone to stop fighting. So, they sit down together to have 'talks.' You can also call them 'negotiations.' These talks are like a big meeting where people try to find a way to make peace. They try to find solutions that make everyone feel safer and happier. It's a bit like when your teacher helps you and your friend solve a problem by talking about it calmly. Did you know? The United Nations is a very big club of almost all the countries in the world, and one of their main jobs is to help countries talk and make peace when there are problems!

Why Are Things 'Stuck'?

The news says that the talks to get a ceasefire are 'stalled.' When something is stalled, it means it's stuck! Like when a car gets stuck in the mud and can't move forward. It means the people talking are finding it very, very hard to agree right now. They haven't been able to make everyone promise to stop fighting yet. It's a little bit tricky, but lots of people are still trying their best to get things moving again, just like you would try hard to push that car out of the mud!

What Are 'Ground Operations' Really?

When we hear 'ground operations,' it means that people from one side are moving around on the land in the southern part of Lebanon. They are trying to do things as part of the big disagreement. It sounds scary, but remember, many people are also trying to keep others safe and make sure the fighting stops. It's important that we don't imagine scary pictures. We just know that people are moving on the ground, and that's why the 'stop fighting' talks are so important, to make sure everyone can be safe and still in their homes. Did you know? Southern Lebanon is a beautiful place with mountains and the sea, just like many places in Africa have amazing nature!

Why is Peace So Important?

We all want peace, don't we? When there is peace, children can go to school, play outside safely, and grow up happy. Families can stay together, and everyone can feel safe in their homes. Peace means sharing and caring. When there's no peace, it can be scary and sad. That's why so many grown-ups around the world are trying very, very hard to help Lebanon and Israel find a way to stop fighting and make peace. We always hope for sunshine after the rain. Did you know? The dove bird is a special sign of peace all around the world!

Let's Keep Hoping for Peace!

So, while things are a little bit stuck right now, many good people are not giving up. They are still talking and trying their very best to help everyone in Lebanon and Israel find a way to live together peacefully. And that's the news for today, my friends! Remember to always try and be kind to your friends, even when you have disagreements. We all wish for peace for everyone, everywhere. This is Kiki the Kind, signing off! Stay curious and kind!
summary: People are trying to help two groups stop fighting in a country called Lebanon, but it's hard to make them agree to a 'time-out' from fighting right now.
whyMatters: It's important because when people fight, it makes many children and families sad and unsafe, so we want peace for everyone.

originalTitle: El Niño under way and threatens weather extremes, scientists say
title: El Niño under way and threatens weather extremes, scientists say
content: An El Niño event has officially started, say US scientists, raising fears of extreme weather and higher temperatures.

title: Deadly Sudan drone strike targets funeral procession
content: Rights groups accuse the RSF paramilitary group of killing civilians in el-Obeid, a city on the civil war's front line.

title: US and Iran exchange strikes across Middle East for second day in a row
content: Iran says it responded to US strikes by targeting American assets in Kuwait, Jordan and Bahrain, which reported damage to its capital city overnight.

title: The rise and rise of 'Meatball' McGinn
content: John McGinn was a boy from Clydebank with a "big, round head" and bigger dreams. Scotland's World Cup opener marks a new high in a storybook career.
